name: AWS Post-Deployment Validation
description: Triggered by CloudWatch 5xx alarm after a Lambda deployment. Runs health checks, fetches CloudWatch metrics, executes integration tests, and produces a GO/NO-GO verdict posted as a GitHub issue.

nodes.name: Notify Validation Started
nodes.description: Notify that post-deployment validation has started

config.message:
## Step 1: Health Check

Run HTTP checks against the API Gateway endpoints.

API Gateway URL: {{variables.api_gw_url}}

For each of the 3 endpoints below, use `execute_local_command` to curl and capture the HTTP status code and response time:
1. GET {{variables.api_gw_url}}/health
2. GET {{variables.api_gw_url}}/api/products
3. POST {{variables.api_gw_url}}/api/checkout  (body: {"item_id": 1, "qty": 1})

For each request use: curl -s -w "\n%{http_code} %{time_total}" -o /tmp/resp_<name>.txt <url>
Then read the status code and latency from the curl output.
For POST: add -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"item_id":1,"qty":1}'

Return JSON ONLY (no markdown):
{
  "health": {"url": "/health", "http_code": 200, "latency_ms": 45, "status": "pass", "body_preview": "..."},
  "products": {"url": "/api/products", "http_code": 200, "latency_ms": 38, "status": "pass", "body_preview": "..."},
  "checkout": {"url": "/api/checkout", "http_code": 500, "latency_ms": 12, "status": "fail", "body_preview": "..."},
  "overall": "fail",
  "failed_endpoints": ["/api/checkout"]
}

## Step 2: CloudWatch 5xx Metrics

Fetch the API Gateway 5xx error metrics for the last 15 minutes.

Alarm triggered at: {{steps.parse_alarm_event.output.alarm_time}}
API Gateway ID: {{variables.api_gw_id}}
AWS Region: {{variables.aws_region}}

Use `aws_cli_execute` with:
- user_id: "{{variables.user_id}}"
- aws_region: "{{variables.aws_region}}"
- aws_access_key_id: "{{variables.aws_access_key_id}}"
- aws_secret_access_key: "{{variables.aws_secret_access_key}}"
- command: fetch `5XXError` metric from namespace `AWS/ApiGateway`
  - dimensions: ApiId={{variables.api_gw_id}}, Stage=$default
  - period: 60 seconds
  - statistics: Sum
  - time window: 15 minutes before alarm_time to 5 minutes after alarm_time

Also fetch `Count` (total requests) in the same window to compute error rate.

Return JSON ONLY:
{
  "total_5xx_errors": 12,
  "total_requests": 15,
  "error_rate_percent": 80.0,
  "peak_errors_per_minute": 5,
  "time_window_start": "2026-04-23T10:00:00Z",
  "time_window_end": "2026-04-23T10:15:00Z",
  "datapoints": [
    {"timestamp": "2026-04-23T10:01:00Z", "5xx_count": 5},
    {"timestamp": "2026-04-23T10:02:00Z", "5xx_count": 7}
  ]
}

## Step 3: Integration Tests

Run the integration test suite against the deployed API.

Use `aws_cli_execute` to invoke the integration test Lambda:
- user_id: "{{variables.user_id}}"
- aws_region: "{{variables.aws_region}}"
- aws_access_key_id: "{{variables.aws_access_key_id}}"
- aws_secret_access_key: "{{variables.aws_secret_access_key}}"
- command: "lambda invoke --function-name demo-5xx-integration-tests --region {{variables.aws_region}} --payload '{\"api_gw_url\": \"{{variables.api_gw_url}}\"}' --cli-binary-format raw-in-base64-out /tmp/test_output.json && cat /tmp/test_output.json"

If lambda invoke fails, fall back to running curl tests directly using `execute_local_command` against each endpoint.

Return JSON ONLY:
{
  "total": 3,
  "passed": 1,
  "failed": 2,
  "overall": "fail",
  "tests": [
    {"name": "health", "http_code": 200, "latency_ms": 45, "status": "pass"},
    {"name": "products", "http_code": 200, "latency_ms": 38, "status": "pass"},
    {"name": "checkout", "http_code": 500, "latency_ms": 12, "status": "fail", "error": "Internal Server Error"}
  ]
}

## Step 4: Validation Analysis

Analyze all validation results and produce a GO/NO-GO verdict.

Here are the collected results:

**Health Check Results:**
{{steps.normalize_validation_results.output.health_check}}

**CloudWatch 5xx Metrics:**
{{steps.normalize_validation_results.output.cloudwatch_metrics}}

**Integration Test Results:**
{{steps.normalize_validation_results.output.integration_tests}}

**Alarm Info:**
{{steps.normalize_validation_results.output.alarm_info}}

VERDICT RULES:
- GO: all health checks pass AND total_5xx_errors == 0 AND all integration tests pass
- NO-GO: any health check fails OR total_5xx_errors > 0 OR any integration test fails
- CONDITIONAL: health checks pass but total_5xx_errors > 0 but < 3 (intermittent)

Return JSON ONLY (no markdown):
{
  "verdict": "NO-GO",
  "confidence": "HIGH",
  "summary": "The /api/checkout endpoint is returning HTTP 500 errors after the latest deployment. 12 5xx errors detected in the last 15 minutes with an 80% error rate.",
  "root_cause": "NullPointerException in checkout handler — cart_service.get_cart() returned None. This was introduced in v2 where the cart service refactor was not propagated to the checkout Lambda.",
  "affected_endpoints": ["/api/checkout"],
  "total_5xx_errors": 12,
  "error_rate_percent": 80.0,
  "recommended_action": "Immediate rollback to stable v1. Run: ./infra/deploy.sh rollback",
  "severity": "HIGH"
}

## Step 5a: Post Validation PASSED Report

Create a GitHub issue documenting the successful validation.

Validation results:
{{steps.ai_validation_analysis.output.output}}

Use `github_create_issue` with:
- user_id: "{{variables.user_id}}"
- github_token: "{{variables.github_token}}"
- repo_url: "{{variables.github_repo_url}}"
- title: "✅ Deployment Validation PASSED — demo-aws-5xx-app"
- labels: ["validation-passed"]
- body: A markdown report including:
  - Verdict: ✅ GO
  - Alarm that triggered: {{steps.parse_alarm_event.output.alarm_name}}
  - Health checks: all passed
  - 5xx error count: 0
  - Integration tests: all passed
  - Validation time: {{steps.normalize_validation_results.output.summary.validation_time}}
  - Summary from AI analysis

Return JSON ONLY:
{"issue_url": "https://github.com/...", "issue_number": 1, "status": "created"}

## Step 5b: Post Validation FAILED Report

Create a GitHub issue documenting the deployment failure.

Validation results:
{{steps.ai_validation_analysis.output.output}}

Use `github_create_issue` with:
- user_id: "{{variables.user_id}}"
- github_token: "{{variables.github_token}}"
- repo_url: "{{variables.github_repo_url}}"
- title: "🚨 Deployment Validation FAILED — demo-aws-5xx-app"
- labels: ["validation-failed", "deployment-failure", "needs-rollback"]
- body: A markdown report with:
  ## 🚨 Deployment Validation FAILED
  **Verdict:** NO-GO | **Severity:** HIGH
  **Alarm:** {{steps.parse_alarm_event.output.alarm_name}}
  **Triggered at:** {{steps.parse_alarm_event.output.alarm_time}}
  
  ## Findings
  - Total 5xx errors: (from analysis)
  - Error rate: (from analysis)
  - Failed endpoints: (from analysis)
  
  ## Root Cause
  (from AI analysis)
  
  ## Health Check Results
  (table of endpoints and status codes)
  
  ## Integration Test Results
  (table of tests)
  
  ## Recommended Action
  (from AI analysis)
  
  ## Rollback Command
  ```bash
  ./infra/deploy.sh rollback
  ```
  
  ---
  *Generated by Aziron Post-Deployment Validation Flow*

Return JSON ONLY:
{"issue_url": "https://github.com/...", "issue_number": 1, "status": "created"}

## Step 6: Publish Validation Result Metric

Publish the validation outcome as a custom CloudWatch metric.

Verdict from analysis: {{steps.ai_validation_analysis.output.output.verdict}}

Use `aws_cli_execute` with:
- user_id: "{{variables.user_id}}"
- aws_region: "{{variables.aws_region}}"
- aws_access_key_id: "{{variables.aws_access_key_id}}"
- aws_secret_access_key: "{{variables.aws_secret_access_key}}"
- command: publish metric to namespace `Aziron/DeployValidation`
  - MetricName: ValidationResult
  - Value: 1 if verdict is GO, 0 if NO-GO or CONDITIONAL
  - Unit: None
  - Dimensions: FunctionName=demo-aws-5xx-app, AlarmName={{steps.parse_alarm_event.output.alarm_name}}

Also publish a second metric:
  - MetricName: ValidationDurationSeconds
  - Value: seconds elapsed since {{steps.parse_alarm_event.output.alarm_time}}
  - Unit: Seconds

Return JSON ONLY:
{"status": "published", "verdict_value": 0, "namespace": "Aziron/DeployValidation"}
